_dryRunNote: This is a DRY RUN receipt. No API calls were made. The admin app (localhost:3000) is not running. This document shows exactly what would be staged when import is executed.

title.incoming: Purify Water with a Portable Filter

requestBody.changeSummary: New action_card guide for chemical water purification tablets (chlorine dioxide, iodine). Extends the Safe Drinking Water fallback chain as a tier-2 backup to boiling. Covers ClO₂ as preferred option (kills Crypto), iodine as secondary (with pregnancy/thyroid warnings), turbid water pre-filtering, and wait time requirements. Sources: EPA, CDC Yellow Book 2024, CDC Emergency Water. Validation: pass_with_warnings — see validation-report.json. Open items: (1) '4 hours' parenthetical is manufacturer-level, reviewer may simplify; (2) no_tablets constraint tag pending code change.

New action_card guide for portable hollow fiber filter water purification (LifeStraw, Sawyer Squeeze). Extends the Safe Drinking Water fallback chain as a tier-2 backup to boiling. Covers bacteria and protozoa removal, critical virus limitation (filters do NOT remove viruses), when to combine with chemical treatment (flood/international), freeze damage warning, NSF/ANSI 53/58 certification guidance. Sources: CDC, WHO. Validation: pass_with_warnings — see validation-report.json. Open items: (1) freeze damage claim is manufacturer-level guidance below authoritative_only tier; (2) no_filter constraint tag pending code change.

validationWarningsForReviewer: purify-water-chemical-tablets: stepByStepActions[3] includes 'typically 4 hours' as a parenthetical — consistent with manufacturer labels (Aquatabs, MSR AquaTabs CIO₂) but not directly sourced from EPA/CDC. Consider simplifying to 'several hours, per the label.'

purify-water-filter: warnings[1] (freeze damage to hollow fiber membranes) is safety-critical and accurate but sourced from manufacturer guidance, not EPA/CDC/WHO at the authoritative_only tier. Reviewer may accept or add source qualifier.

Both guides: blockedByConstraints is empty — requires new constraint tags (no_tablets, no_filter) to be added to constraint-tags.ts before the fallback chain can properly gate these guides when those resources are unavailable.

postImportActions.action: Add no_tablets and no_filter to constraint-tags.ts
postImportActions.description: Add two new constraint tags to the CONSTRAINT_TAGS array. Then update purify-water-bleach via the backfill script to add no_tablets and no_filter to its constraintTags, pushing bleach to last-resort position in the fallback chain.
This guide is a duplicate of purify-water-bleach. Both hold backup role with identical constraint metadata. Archive the duplicate to clean up the fallback chain.
Review both new draft guides in the Review Queue. Approve to move to approved status. Approved guides can then be included in a release.

toRunImport: Start the admin app with 'npm run dev' in PrepKit Admin/prepkit-admin/, then call the preview and save API endpoints for each guide. Verify both appear in the Review Queue at http://localhost:3000/review?filter=draft. Do NOT include them in a release until a human reviews and approves them.
